decisions.summary: Use Bun + TypeScript strict + React Router v7 on Cloudflare Workers for the launch baseline.
Initialize the repository with main as the default branch and use branch-per-change after bootstrap.
Launch with guest checkout, signed report access links, and no end-user account system in v1.
Position the product as AI-driven Eastern life guidance and self-reflection, not fortune telling or psychic prediction.

projectSkillBlueprints.description: Guide the English-market growth funnel from free mini reading through paid full report and upsell.
whenToUse: Use when landing pages, offer packaging, pricing, conversion steps, or upsell flow change.
Use when deciding what belongs in the launch funnel versus later subscription phases.
workflow: Treat the launch loop as free mini reading -> preview paywall -> paid full report -> follow-up Q&A -> 90-day upsell.
Keep launch scope centered on the Core Personality + Next 90 Days report and defer subscription mechanics unless explicitly requested.
Prefer conversion clarity, short time-to-value, and low-friction guest checkout over community or account-system complexity.
guardrails: Do not expand launch scope into broad spiritual marketplace features.
Do not frame the offer around guaranteed prediction, destiny claims, or fixed-fate language.

projectSkillBlueprints.description: Keep copy, UX, and prompts aligned with AI disclosure and self-reflection positioning guardrails.
whenToUse: Use when changing public copy, checkout wording, prompts, or report content policy.
Use when adding new pages, legal content, or region-specific risk handling.
workflow: Require AI disclosure, entertainment and self-reflection disclaimers, and no medical, legal, or financial advice language on relevant user-facing flows.
Prefer terms such as pattern-based insight, timing guidance, and reflection prompts over fortune telling language.
Block or flag copy that implies guaranteed outcomes, removing bad luck, fixed destiny, or prohibited regulated advice.
guardrails: Do not use public-facing claims that promise accuracy, guaranteed outcomes, or supernatural certainty.
Do not add high-risk features such as health diagnosis, face reading, or regulated decision-making.

projectSkillBlueprints.description: Keep generated reading quality stable through fixed structure, tone rules, and output validation.
whenToUse: Use when changing prompts, schemas, preview/full report behavior, or follow-up question handling.
workflow: Keep readings in a fixed seven-section structure: opening summary, strengths, recurring challenges, relationship style, work pattern, this season guidance, reflection prompts.
Separate preview and full-reading generation rules and validate that required sections, disclaimers, and tone constraints are present.
Bias toward grounded reflective coaching language with light mystical flavor, not therapy imitation or absolute prediction.
guardrails: Do not allow prompt outputs that omit required disclaimers or banned phrase checks.
Do not let free-form LLM output define the product contract; schemas and validators remain the source of truth.

projectSkillBlueprints.description: Guide Cloudflare-first service boundaries, bindings, and deployment conventions for the SaaS starter.
whenToUse: Use when editing runtime architecture, wrangler configuration, data bindings, or local dev and deploy commands.
workflow: Keep the launch architecture Cloudflare-first with Workers, D1, KV, R2, Queues, Turnstile, Analytics Engine, and AI Gateway.
Use repo-local Wrangler configuration and generated types instead of relying on global CLI state or handwritten Env interfaces.
Defer Workflows and more complex platform features until a clear post-launch need exists.
guardrails: Do not introduce external infrastructure for core runtime responsibilities unless a concrete blocker exists.
Do not hand-write binding types when Wrangler-generated types are available.

notes: Launch market defaults are US, UK, CA, and AU with English-only copy and USD pricing.
The v1 runtime should stay Cloudflare-first and only leave payment and llm providers as unfinished production adapters.
Use guest checkout and signed delivery links instead of end-user accounts for the launch version.
